rationale: Lands the three bounded contexts (CRM, Ticket, AI) the body promises across 33 files in a textbook DDD layout: `internal/domain/{ai,crm,ticket}/{entity,errors,mock_repo,repository,service,service_test}.go` + MySQL implementations under `internal/infrastructure/mysql/` + HTTP handlers under `internal/interfaces/http/handler/`. The CRM `Customer` entity ships multi-phone support via the `customer_phones` relation table with `mu sync.RWMutex` guards on the mock repo and explicit primary-phone validation. The Ticket BC implements the documented state machine with mock and MySQL backends. The AI BC adds `KnowledgeCategory`, `KnowledgeArticle` with full-text search, `AgentScript`, and `SessionInfoTemplate`. The PR-#8 bug fixes are visible: `svc.Update(ctx, s)` is wired into the campaign update test/handler, and `internal/domain/telephony/service.go` adds `mu sync.RWMutex` plus uses `s.mu.Lock()/Unlock()` in `RecordHealthCheck` and `s.mu.RLock()/RUnlock()` in `GetHealthStatus`, with the latter returning a snapshot via `cp := *h; return &cp` to prevent races on the shared pointer (matching the PR-#9 review fix). The PR-#9 review fix to add JSON struct tags to input structs is visible in the entity files. Application-layer `screenpop` is extended to match by caller phone and return interaction history. The diff is large (3492 lines per the body) but coherent and matches every enumerated promise; the test count of 154 is plausible given the per-BC `service_test.go` files alongside existing tests.
